message:
cli: add unit tests for pure helper functions (bd-2jc9t)

Test 6 previously untested pure functions across assign.go, send.go,
spawn.go, and pipeline.go. CLI coverage: 28.6% → 28.9%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -48,6 +48,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-16yf","title":"E2E Tests: ntm assign full workflow with real agents","description":"## Test Coverage
Comprehensive end-to-end test of ntm assign with real tmux and agents, covering all assignment features.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Basic Assignment Flow
1. Create test workspace with beads
2. Spawn test agents (--cc=2)
3. Run ntm assign
4. Verify assignments distributed
5. Verify prompts received by agents
6. Verify assignment tracking state

### 2. Strategy Testing
- Test --strategy=balanced (default)
- Test --strategy=speed
- Test --strategy=quality
- Test --strategy=dependency
- Test --strategy=round-robin

### 3. Pane-Specific Assignment (--pane)
1. Spawn agents
2. Run ntm assign --pane=2 bd-xyz
3. Verify only pane 2 got assignment
4. Test --force flag for busy agents
5. Test --ignore-deps for blocked beads

### 4. Reassignment (--reassign)
1. Assign bead to agent 1
2. Run ntm assign --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=3
3. Verify old assignment marked "reassigned"
4. Verify new assignment created
5. Verify file reservations transferred

### 5. Clear Assignment (--clear)
1. Assign beads to agents
2. Run ntm assign --clear bd-xyz
3. Verify assignment removed
4. Verify file reservations released
5. Test --clear-pane for bulk clear
6. Test --clear-failed for failed assignments

### 6. Combined spawn --assign
1. Run ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --assign
2. Verify agents spawned AND assignments created
3. Test with --strategy flag

## Detailed Logging Requirements

### Log Format Standard
```
[TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [COMPONENT] Message
Example: [2026-01-19T12:00:00Z] [INFO] [SETUP] Creating test workspace
```

### Log Levels
- DEBUG: Detailed command output, intermediate state
- INFO: Test step progress, milestones
- WARN: Non-fatal issues, skipped assertions
- ERROR: Test failures, assertion failures
- PASS: Successful assertions/test completion

### Required Log Points

#### Setup Phase
```
[INFO] [SETUP] Creating test workspace at /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$
[DEBUG] [SETUP] Running: mkdir -p /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$/beads
[INFO] [SETUP] Creating 5 test beads with dependencies
[DEBUG] [SETUP] Bead bd-test-1: "Test feature A"
[INFO] [SETUP] Workspace ready: /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$
```

#### Spawn Phase
```
[INFO] [SPAWN] Spawning 2 Claude agents
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Running: ntm spawn $SESSION --cc=2
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Output: {"session":"$SESSION","agents":[...]}
[INFO] [SPAWN] Waiting for agents to become ready...
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Pane 2 status: ready
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Pane 3 status: ready
[INFO] [SPAWN] All 2 agents ready
```

#### Test Execution Phase
```
[INFO] [TEST:basic_assign] Starting basic assignment test
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Running: ntm assign $SESSION --auto
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Output: {"strategy":"balanced","assigned":[...]}
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Expected: 2 assignments
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Actual: 2 assignments
[PASS] [TEST:basic_assign] Assignment count matches
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Checking pane 2 assignment...
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Expected: bd-test-1
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Actual: bd-test-1
[PASS] [TEST:basic_assign] Pane 2 assignment correct
[PASS] [TEST:basic_assign] Basic assignment test PASSED
```

#### Assertion Format
```
[DEBUG] [ASSERT] Checking: pane 2 should have assignment bd-test-1
[DEBUG] [ASSERT] Expected: bd-test-1
[DEBUG] [ASSERT] Actual: bd-test-1
[PASS] [ASSERT] Assertion passed
```

#### Failure Logging
```
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Assertion failed: file reservation transfer
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Expected: file_reservations_transferred=true
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Actual: file_reservations_transferred=false
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Context: Previous pane=2, Target pane=3
[DEBUG] [TEST:reassign] Assignment store state: {...}
[DEBUG] [TEST:reassign] Agent Mail reservation state: {...}
[FAIL] [TEST:reassign] Test failed at step 5
```

#### Cleanup Phase
```
[INFO] [CLEANUP] Killing test session $SESSION
[DEBUG] [CLEANUP] Running: ntm kill $SESSION
[INFO] [CLEANUP] Removing test workspace
[DEBUG] [CLEANUP] Running: rm -rf /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$
[INFO] [CLEANUP] Cleanup complete
```

### Log File Structure
```
/tmp/ntm-e2e-assign-full-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.log
├── Header (test info, timestamp, version)
├── Setup section
├── Test sections (one per test suite)
│   ├── Test name
│   ├── Steps with DEBUG/INFO
│   ├── Assertions with PASS/FAIL
│   └── Test result
├── Summary section
│   ├── Total tests
│   ├── Passed
│   ├── Failed
│   └── Skipped
└── Cleanup section
```

## Test Script Structure
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
LOG_FILE=/tmp/ntm-e2e-assign-full-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log
SESSION="ntm-e2e-test-$$"
TEST_DIR="/tmp/ntm-e2e-$$"
VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-0}

# Logging functions
log_debug() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [DEBUG] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_info()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_warn()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [WARN] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_error() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [ERROR] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" >&2; }
log_pass()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [PASS] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_fail()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [FAIL] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" >&2; }

assert_eq() {
    local desc="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
    log_debug "[ASSERT] Checking: $desc"
    log_debug "[ASSERT] Expected: $expected"
    log_debug "[ASSERT] Actual: $actual"
    if [[ "$expected" == "$actual" ]]; then
        log_pass "[ASSERT] $desc"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "[ASSERT] $desc"
        log_error "[ASSERT] Expected: $expected, Got: $actual"
        return 1
    fi
}

run_cmd() {
    local cmd="$1"
    log_debug "[CMD] Running: $cmd"
    local output
    output=$(eval "$cmd" 2>&1) || {
        log_error "[CMD] Command failed: $cmd"
        log_error "[CMD] Output: $output"
        return 1
    }
    log_debug "[CMD] Output: $output"
    echo "$output"
}

# ... test functions ...

main() {
    log_info "=========================================="
    log_info "NTM Assign E2E Tests - $(date)"
    log_info "Session: $SESSION"
    log_info "=========================================="
    
    # Run tests, summarize results
}

trap cleanup EXIT
main
```

## Test File
tests/e2e/test_assign_full.sh

## Dependencies
This test depends on all ntm assign features being implemented:
- bd-k3zq (ntm assign)
- bd-1gbt5 (--reassign)
- bd-2a5yf (--clear)
- bd-2fxyp (--pane)
- bd-3nde (spawn --assign)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:30.116015338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T04:55:11.728385613Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T04:55:11.728331Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive E2E tests for ntm assign workflow: TestAssign_BasicFlow, TestAssign_Strategies (5 strategies), TestAssign_PaneSpecific, TestAssign_Reassignment, TestAssign_Clear, TestAssign_CombinedSpawnAssign, TestAssign_StateTracking, TestAssign_NonexistentSession, TestAssign_DryRun.  {"id":"bd-17xu8","title":"Task: Implement early stopping based on marginal utility","description":"Stop ensemble early when new findings rate drops.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents (default: 3)
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents (default: 2)
    Enabled             bool    // Can be disabled
}

type EarlyStopEvaluator struct {
    Config   *EarlyStopConfig
    History  []AgentMetrics
}

type AgentMetrics struct {
    AgentIndex    int
    TokensSpent   int
    FindingsCount int
    SimilarityToOthers float64
}

func NewEarlyStopEvaluator(config *EarlyStopConfig) *EarlyStopEvaluator
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) RecordAgent(metrics AgentMetrics)
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) ShouldStop() (bool, string)
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) GetFindingsVelocity() float64
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) GetAverageSimilarity() float64
```

## Stop Conditions
- Findings velocity drops below threshold for WindowSize agents
- Output similarity exceeds threshold (redundant agents)
- Both conditions evaluated after MinAgentsBeforeStop

 {"id":"bd-17zih","title":"PromptInjector: Staggered sending with configurable delays","description":"## Purpose
Implement staggered prompt sending with configurable delays to prevent rate limiting when injecting marching orders to multiple agent panes simultaneously.

## Implementation Details

```go
// StaggeredSender handles rate-limited prompt injection
type StaggeredSender struct {
    tracker    *ratelimit.RateLimitTracker
    tmux       *tmux.Client
    baseDelay  time.Duration
    logger     *slog.Logger
}

// SendStaggered sends prompts to multiple panes with adaptive delays
func (s *StaggeredSender) SendStaggered(ctx context.Context, injections []PaneInjection) error {
    for i, inj := range injections {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return ctx.Err()
        default:
        }
        
        // Get adaptive delay from rate limit tracker
        delay := s.tracker.GetOptimalDelay(inj.AgentType)
        if i > 0 {
            s.logger.Info("staggered delay before send",
                "pane", inj.PaneID,
                "delay_ms", delay.Milliseconds(),
                "index", i,
                "total", len(injections))
            time.Sleep(delay)
        }
        
        // Send the prompt
        if err := s.tmux.SendKeys(inj.PaneID, inj.Prompt, true); err != nil {
            s.logger.Error("failed to send prompt",
                "pane", inj.PaneID,
                "error", err)
            return fmt.Errorf("send to pane %s: %w", inj.PaneID, err)
        }
        
        s.logger.Info("prompt sent successfully",
            "pane", inj.PaneID,
            "agent_type", inj.AgentType,
            "prompt_len", len(inj.Prompt))
        
        // Record success for adaptive delay learning
        s.tracker.RecordSuccess(inj.AgentType)
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log delay duration before each send (milliseconds)
- Log pane index and total count for progress tracking
- Log prompt length (not content) on successful send
- Log errors with full context

## Integration with Existing Code
- MUST use internal/ratelimit.RateLimitTracker for adaptive delays
- Leverage existing GetOptimalDelay() and RecordSuccess() methods

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Configurable base delay with per-provider overrides
- [ ] Adaptive delay learning via RateLimitTracker integration
- [ ] Context cancellation support for clean shutdown
- [ ] Structured logging at each step
- [ ] No prompt content in logs (security)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:34.776957381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:48:35.935330695Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:48:35.935240616Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17zih","depends_on_id":"bd-25jgi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-17zih","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2iw6e","title":"Task: Agent Output Pattern Parser (Foundation)","description":"## Task: Agent Output Pattern Parser (Foundation)

### Why This Task Exists

This is the FOUNDATION for all usage limit detection in NTM. Without accurate parsing of agent output text, we cannot distinguish between:
- An agent actively writing code (DO NOT INTERRUPT)
- An agent waiting at a prompt (safe to restart)
- An agent that hit a rate limit (wait for reset)
- An agent with low context (proactive restart candidate)

The user was EMPHATIC: **"NEVER interrupt agents doing useful work\!\!\!"**

This parser makes that possible by extracting structured state from raw terminal output.

### Background: How Agent Output Reaches Us

```
Agent (cc/cod/gmi) 
    │
    ▼
Tmux Pane Buffer (scrollback)
    │
    ▼
ntm --robot-tail=session --panes=N --lines=100
    │
    ▼
Raw text output (this is what we parse)
    │
    ▼
Parser Module (THIS TASK)
    │
    ▼
Structured AgentState object
```

### Agent-Specific Pattern Knowledge

#### Claude Code (cc) Patterns

```go
// RATE LIMIT PATTERNS (highest priority - agent is blocked)
var ccRateLimitPatterns = []string{
    `You.ve hit your limit`,
    `Rate limit exceeded`,
    `Please wait`,
    `try again later`,
    `too many requests`,
}

// CONTEXT WARNING PATTERNS (agent running low)
var ccContextWarningPatterns = []string{
    `This conversation is getting long`,
    `context.*limit`,
    `running out of context`,
}

// WORKING PATTERNS (agent actively producing - DO NOT INTERRUPT)
var ccWorkingPatterns = []string{
    "```",                    // Code block (start or end)
    `Writing to `,            // File write operation
    `Created `,               // File creation
    `Modified `,              // File modification  
    `Reading `,               // File read operation
    `Searching `,             // Search in progress
    `Running `,               // Command execution
    `Thinking`,               // Processing indicator
}

// IDLE PATTERNS (agent waiting for input - safe to restart)
var ccIdlePatterns = []string{
    `>\s*$`,                  // Prompt waiting for input
    `Human:\s*$`,             // Conversation prompt
}
```

#### Codex CLI (cod) Patterns

```go
// CONTEXT PERCENTAGE (CRITICAL - Codex shows this explicitly\!)
// Example: "47% context left · ? for shortcuts"
var codContextPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(\d+)%\s*context\s*left`)

// TOKEN USAGE (appears after each response)
// Example: "Token usage: total=219,582 input=206,150 (+ 9,074,560 cached) output=13,432 (reasoning 7,744)"
var codTokenUsagePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`Token usage:\s*total=(\d+)`)

// RATE LIMIT PATTERNS
var codRateLimitPatterns = []string{
    `You.ve reached your usage limit`,
    `Rate limit exceeded`,
    `quota exceeded`,
}

// WORKING PATTERNS
var codWorkingPatterns = []string{
    "```",                    // Code block
    `Editing `,               // File edit
    `Creating `,              // File creation
    `$ `,                     // Command execution
}
```

#### Gemini CLI (gmi) Patterns

```go
// MEMORY USAGE (less precise than Codex percentage)
// Example: "gemini-3-pro-preview /model | 396.8 MB"
var gmiMemoryPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(\d+\.?\d*)\s*MB`)

// YOLO MODE (affects execution behavior)
var gmiYoloPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`YOLO mode:\s*(ON|OFF)`)

// RATE LIMIT PATTERNS (less explicit, need heuristics)
var gmiRateLimitPatterns = []string{
    `error`,
    `failed`,
    `quota`,
    `limit`,
}

// SHELL MODE INDICATOR (GOTCHA: triggered by "\!" in prompts)
var gmiShellModePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^\s*\!\s*`)
```

### Data Structures

```go
package agent

import "time"

// AgentType identifies the agent CLI being used
type AgentType string

const (
    AgentTypeClaudeCode AgentType = "cc"
    AgentTypeCodex      AgentType = "cod"
    AgentTypeGemini     AgentType = "gmi"
    AgentTypeUnknown    AgentType = "unknown"
)

// AgentState represents parsed state from agent output
type AgentState struct {
    // Core identification
    Type      AgentType `json:"agent_type"`
    ParsedAt  time.Time `json:"parsed_at"`
    
    // Context/usage metrics (nil if unknown)
    ContextRemaining *float64 `json:"context_remaining,omitempty"` // 0-100 percentage
    TokensUsed       *int64   `json:"tokens_used,omitempty"`
    MemoryMB         *float64 `json:"memory_mb,omitempty"`
    
    // State flags
    IsWorking    bool `json:"is_working"`     // Actively producing output
    IsRateLimited bool `json:"rate_limited"`  // Hit usage limit
    IsContextLow bool `json:"context_low"`    // Below threshold (configurable)
    IsIdle       bool `json:"is_idle"`        // Waiting for input
    IsInError    bool `json:"is_in_error"`    // Error state detected
    
    // Evidence (for debugging/confidence)
    WorkIndicators  []string `json:"work_indicators,omitempty"`
    LimitIndicators []string `json:"limit_indicators,omitempty"`
    RawSample       string   `json:"raw_sample,omitempty"` // Last N chars for debugging
    
    // Confidence score (0.0-1.0)
    Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"`
}

// Recommendation based on parsed state
type Recommendation string

const (
    RecommendDoNotInterrupt    Recommendation = "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT"
    RecommendSafeToRestart     Recommendation = "SAFE_TO_RESTART"
    RecommendContextLowContinue Recommendation = "CONTEXT_LOW_CONTINUE"
    RecommendRateLimitedWait   Recommendation = "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT"
    RecommendErrorState        Recommendation = "ERROR_STATE"
    RecommendUnknown           Recommendation = "UNKNOWN"
)

// GetRecommendation derives action from state
func (s *AgentState) GetRecommendation() Recommendation {
    if s.IsRateLimited {
        return RecommendRateLimitedWait
    }
    if s.IsInError {
        return RecommendErrorState
    }
    if s.IsWorking {
        if s.IsContextLow {
            return RecommendContextLowContinue
        }
        return RecommendDoNotInterrupt
    }
    if s.IsIdle {
        return RecommendSafeToRestart
    }
    return RecommendUnknown
}
```

### Parser Interface

```go
package agent

// Parser extracts AgentState from raw terminal output
type Parser interface {
    // Parse analyzes output and returns structured state
    Parse(output string) (*AgentState, error)
    
    // DetectAgentType identifies agent from output patterns
    DetectAgentType(output string) AgentType
}

// NewParser creates parser with default configuration
func NewParser() Parser {
    return &parserImpl{
        contextLowThreshold: 20.0, // Flag if below 20%
        sampleLength:        500,  // Chars to keep in RawSample
    }
}

// Config allows customizing parser behavior
type ParserConfig struct {
    ContextLowThreshold float64 // Percentage below which to flag
    SampleLength        int     // Chars to keep for debugging
}

func NewParserWithConfig(cfg ParserConfig) Parser {
    return &parserImpl{
        contextLowThreshold: cfg.ContextLowThreshold,
        sampleLength:        cfg.SampleLength,
    }
}
```

### Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/agent/types.go` | AgentType, AgentState, Recommendation types |
| `internal/agent/patterns.go` | Regex patterns for each agent type |
| `internal/agent/parser.go` | Parser implementation |
| `internal/agent/parser_test.go` | Unit tests with real output samples |
| `internal/agent/testdata/` | Sample outputs for testing |

### Confidence Scoring Logic

```go
func (p *parserImpl) calculateConfidence(state *AgentState, matches int, totalChecks int) float64 {
    base := float64(matches) / float64(totalChecks)
    
    // Boost confidence for explicit indicators
    if state.ContextRemaining \!= nil {
        base += 0.2 // Codex gives us explicit percentage
    }
    if len(state.WorkIndicators) > 2 {
        base += 0.1 // Multiple work indicators = high confidence
    }
    if len(state.LimitIndicators) > 0 {
        base += 0.15 // Rate limit messages are unambiguous
    }
    
    // Cap at 1.0
    if base > 1.0 {
        base = 1.0
    }
    return base
}
```

### Testing Strategy

1. **Unit tests with real output samples**: Capture actual agent output during various states
2. **Regression tests**: Ensure pattern changes do not break existing detection
3. **Fuzzing**: Test with malformed/unexpected output
4. **Integration tests**: Verify parser works with real --robot-tail output

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detects all three agent types (cc, cod, gmi)
- [ ] Extracts context percentage from Codex output
- [ ] Identifies rate limit messages for all agent types
- [ ] Distinguishes working vs idle states
- [ ] Provides confidence scores
- [ ] Returns actionable Recommendation
- [ ] Unit tests cover edge cases
- [ ] Real output samples in testdata/

### Why This Must Be Done First

Every other task in this EPIC depends on the parser:
- `--robot-is-working` calls parser to determine state
- `--robot-agent-health` calls parser + caut integration
- `--robot-smart-restart` refuses to act without parser confirmation
- Warning system monitors parsed state over time

Without accurate parsing, all downstream features are unreliable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:34:42.462914128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T01:53:59.284381177Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T01:53:59.284326705Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete: types (bd-120q6), patterns (bd-3pnw7), parser core (bd-2bsmx), testdata (bd-1myil). +package cli
+
+import (
+	"strings"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/bv"
+)
+
+// =============================================================================
+// assign.go helpers (not tested in assign_test.go)

+
+func TestAssignmentAgentName(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		name      string
+		session   string
+		agentType string
+		paneIndex int
+		want      string
+	}{
+		{"normal", "dev-session", "claude", 0, "dev-session_claude_0"},
+		{"codex pane 3", "prod", "codex", 3, "prod_codex_3"},
+		{"empty session", "", "claude", 0, ""},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range tests {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			t.Parallel()
+			got := assignmentAgentName(tc.session, tc.agentType, tc.paneIndex)
+			if got != tc.want {
+				t.Errorf("assignmentAgentName(%q, %q, %d) = %q; want %q",
+					tc.session, tc.agentType, tc.paneIndex, got, tc.want)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestCalculateMatchConfidence(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		name      string
+		agentType string
+		bead      bv.BeadPreview
+		strategy  string
+		wantMin   float64
+		wantMax   float64
+	}{
+		{
+			name:      "claude analysis task",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Analyze the codebase for issues"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantMin:   0.85,
+			wantMax:   1.0,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "codex feature task",
+			agentType: "codex",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Implement user authentication feature"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantMin:   0.85,
+			wantMax:   1.0,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "gemini docs task",
+			agentType: "gemini",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Update documentation for API"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantMin:   0.85,
+			wantMax:   1.0,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "unknown agent generic task",
+			agentType: "unknown",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Do some work"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantMin:   0.6,
+			wantMax:   0.8,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "speed strategy raises confidence",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Generic task"},
+			strategy:  "speed",
+			wantMin:   0.75,
+			wantMax:   0.95,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "dependency strategy with P0",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Fix critical bug", Priority: "P0"},
+			strategy:  "dependency",
+			wantMin:   0.75,
+			wantMax:   0.95,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "dependency strategy with P1",
+			agentType: "codex",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Fix high-priority bug", Priority: "P1"},
+			strategy:  "dependency",
+			wantMin:   0.75,
+			wantMax:   0.95,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "codex bug task",
+			agentType: "codex",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Fix broken login bug"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantMin:   0.75,
+			wantMax:   0.85,
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range tests {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			t.Parallel()
+			got := calculateMatchConfidence(tc.agentType, tc.bead, tc.strategy)
+			if got < tc.wantMin || got > tc.wantMax {
+				t.Errorf("calculateMatchConfidence(%q, %q, %q) = %f; want [%f, %f]",
+					tc.agentType, tc.bead.Title, tc.strategy, got, tc.wantMin, tc.wantMax)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestBuildReasoning(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		name      string
+		agentType string
+		bead      bv.BeadPreview
+		strategy  string
+		wantSub   string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:      "claude refactor",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Refactor the auth module"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantSub:   "Claude excels",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "codex implement",
+			agentType: "codex",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Implement new feature"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantSub:   "Codex excels",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "gemini docs",
+			agentType: "gemini",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Update documentation"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantSub:   "Gemini excels",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "P0 priority",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Something", Priority: "P0"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantSub:   "critical priority",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "P1 priority",
+			agentType: "codex",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Something", Priority: "P1"},
+			strategy:  "balanced",
+			wantSub:   "high priority",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "speed strategy",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Something"},
+			strategy:  "speed",
+			wantSub:   "optimizing for speed",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "quality strategy",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Something"},
+			strategy:  "quality",
+			wantSub:   "optimizing for quality",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "dependency strategy",
+			agentType: "claude",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Something"},
+			strategy:  "dependency",
+			wantSub:   "prioritizing unblocks",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "no special match",
+			agentType: "unknown",
+			bead:      bv.BeadPreview{Title: "Something", Priority: "P3"},
+			strategy:  "",
+			wantSub:   "available agent matched",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range tests {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			t.Parallel()
+			got := buildReasoning(tc.agentType, tc.bead, tc.strategy)
+			if !strings.Contains(got, tc.wantSub) {
+				t.Errorf("buildReasoning(%q, %q, %q) = %q; want substring %q",
+					tc.agentType, tc.bead.Title, tc.strategy, got, tc.wantSub)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// =============================================================================

+func TestNormalizeCommandLine(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		input string
+		want  string
+	}{
+		{"$ ls -la", "ls -la"},
+		{"> git status", "git status"},
+		{"# echo hello", "echo hello"},
+		{"  $ npm install  ", "npm install"},
+		{"plain command", "plain command"},
+		{"", ""},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range tests {
+		t.Run(tc.input, func(t *testing.T) {
+			t.Parallel()
+			got := normalizeCommandLine(tc.input)
+			if got != tc.want {
+				t.Errorf("normalizeCommandLine(%q) = %q; want %q", tc.input, got, tc.want)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}

+
+func TestResolveSpawnAssignAgentType(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		name                     string
+		agent                    string
+		ccOnly, codOnly, gmiOnly bool
+		want                     string
+	}{
+		{"explicit agent", "Claude", false, false, false, "claude"},
+		{"cc only flag", "", true, false, false, "claude"},
+		{"cod only flag", "", false, true, false, "codex"},
+		{"gmi only flag", "", false, false, true, "gemini"},
+		{"no agent no flags", "", false, false, false, ""},
+		{"agent takes precedence", "codex", true, false, false, "codex"},
+		{"whitespace agent", "  ", false, false, false, ""},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range tests {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			t.Parallel()
+			got := resolveSpawnAssignAgentType(tc.agent, tc.ccOnly, tc.codOnly, tc.gmiOnly)
+			if got != tc.want {
+				t.Errorf("resolveSpawnAssignAgentType(%q, %v, %v, %v) = %q; want %q",
+					tc.agent, tc.ccOnly, tc.codOnly, tc.gmiOnly, got, tc.want)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// pipeline.go helpers
+// =============================================================================
+
+func TestParseDurationCLI(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	tests := []struct {
+		name    string
+		input   string
+		want    time.Duration
+		wantErr bool
+	}{
+		{"days", "7d", 7 * 24 * time.Hour, false},
+		{"single day", "1d", 24 * time.Hour, false},
+		{"weeks", "2w", 14 * 24 * time.Hour, false},
+		{"hours fallback", "24h", 24 * time.Hour, false},
+		{"minutes fallback", "30m", 30 * time.Minute, false},
+		{"empty", "", 0, true},
+		{"whitespace", "  ", 0, true},
+		{"invalid day", "abcd", 0, true},
+		{"invalid week", "abcw", 0, true},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range tests {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			t.Parallel()
+			got, err := parseDuration(tc.input)
+			if tc.wantErr {
+				if err == nil {
+					t.Errorf("parseDuration(%q) want error, got nil", tc.input)
+				}
+				return
+			}
+			if err != nil {
+				t.Fatalf("parseDuration(%q) unexpected error: %v", tc.input, err)
+			}
+			if got != tc.want {
+				t.Errorf("parseDuration(%q) = %v; want %v", tc.input, got, tc.want)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
